title: Platform-level company provisioning and administration
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want to provision companies, manage supported jurisdictions, define global groups, and maintain platform-wide configurations, so that new tenants can be onboarded and operated on a shared SaaS platform.
acceptance_criteria: Given I hold platform-level administration rights, when I create a company, then it is provisioned as a new tenant with the selected supported jurisdictions.; Given an existing tenant, when I suspend or reactivate it, then its operational status changes accordingly.; Given a jurisdictions catalog, when I add or update supported jurisdictions, then they become available for company setup.; Given platform-wide configurations, when I define global groups and configurations, then they apply across the platform where applicable.; Given tenant onboarding activity, when I monitor onboarding and support, then I can track tenant status and view platform health and logs.

title: Enable authentication methods and identity integrations
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want to enable authentication methods and configure identity integrations (SSO providers), so that tenants can authenticate securely using supported methods.
acceptance_criteria: Given platform administration access, when I configure SSO providers, then those authentication methods become available to tenants.; Given identity integrations for SAML, AD, Office 365, and Google Apps, when I enable an integration, then it can be used for tenant authentication.; Given Phase 1 scope, when I review available integrations, then only authentication providers are supported (no other external integrations).
source: FR 4.1 (authentication methods and identity integrations); FR 8.10.1 (SAML, AD, Office 365, Google Apps)

title: Portfolio administration across multiple companies
user_story: As a Portfolio Admin, I want to administer multiple companies within my assigned portfolio and reuse common setup patterns, policies, and administrative processes, so that shared services teams can operate several companies efficiently and consistently.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am an authorized portfolio manager, when I access my portfolio, then I can administer the companies assigned to me subject to security controls.; Given common setup patterns and policies, when I apply them across managed companies, then standardization is enforced across the portfolio companies.; Given company-level admin tasks, when I perform them within the portfolio, then I can act only as permitted per company.; Given cross-company administration, when I run oversight and reporting, then access is restricted by my authorization scope.

title: Switch company context without re-authentication
user_story: As a Portfolio Admin, I want to switch operational context between authorized companies through a single login without repeated authentication, so that I can work across companies without friction.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am authenticated once, when I switch to another authorized company, then I am not prompted to re-authenticate.; Given I switch context, when I operate in the new company, then only that company's data and my permitted actions there are available.; Given I attempt to switch to a company outside my authorization, when the switch is requested, then access is denied.

title: Group-company relationship governance
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want to define and maintain group-company relationships and oversee related companies within an approved group structure, so that affiliated entities can have consolidated visibility and governance subject to authorization.
acceptance_criteria: Given affiliated entities, when I define a group-company relationship, then related companies are linked under the approved group structure.; Given a group structure, when I enable consolidated visibility, then governance across affiliated companies is available subject to authorization.; Given shared administrative constructs and shared locations (if enabled), when I configure them, then they apply across the approved group with cross-company sharing approval.; Given cross-company sharing, when it is requested, then it proceeds only after approval.

title: Company-level setup and administration
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to maintain company details and define departments, positions, groups, and locations, so that the tenant's organizational structure is established for HR operations.
acceptance_criteria: Given company administration access, when I maintain company details, then updated details are saved for the tenant.; Given the tenant structure, when I define departments, positions, groups, and locations, then they are available for employee and policy assignment.; Given a Company Super Administrator, when I configure the tenant, then I have ultimate authority within the tenant for setup, organizational structures, and configuration.

title: Company-level roles, permissions, and operations
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to manage employees, apply policies and workflows, assign roles and permissions, and run reports and bulk data operations, so that the company can be fully operated within its tenant.
acceptance_criteria: Given company administration access, when I assign roles and permissions to users, then those users gain the corresponding capabilities.; Given policies and workflows, when I apply them, then they take effect for the company's employees.; Given a Company HR Administrator, when I manage employees, policies, lifecycle workflows, and leave/attendance, then I can do so without tenant-wide technical control.; Given reporting and bulk data tools, when I run reports or bulk operations, then results are produced within the company's tenant scope.

title: Tenant data isolation
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want each company's business data to be logically isolated from other tenants, so that data is protected except where cross-company access is explicitly enabled.
acceptance_criteria: Given two independent companies, when a user of one accesses data, then data of the other company is not visible.; Given explicitly enabled multi-company user access, portfolio administration, or group-company relationships, when cross-company access is authorized, then only the permitted shared data is accessible.; Given any data access, when it occurs, then tenant boundaries and authorized cross-company access rules are always respected.

title: Enforce single portfolio / single group membership
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want a company to belong to at most one portfolio and at most one group-company structure at a time, so that administrative and governance relationships remain unambiguous.
acceptance_criteria: Given a company already assigned to a portfolio, when I attempt to assign it to a second portfolio, then the action is rejected.; Given a company already in a group-company structure, when I attempt to add it to a second group structure, then the action is rejected.; Given a company, when it is set up, then it may operate standalone, within a group structure, or under a portfolio construct (exactly one of these arrangements at a time).

title: Roles separate from composable permission sets
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want roles to represent personas while permissions represent composable capabilities, so that the same permission set can be reused across multiple roles.
acceptance_criteria: Given a permission set, when I assign it, then it can be attached to multiple roles.; Given a role, when I define it, then it is composed of one or more permission sets rather than hard-coded capabilities.; Given a persona, when I model it as a role, then changing a permission set updates every role that includes it.

title: Contextual role assignment per company
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want a single user to hold different roles in different companies, so that people working across multiple tenants get the correct access in each context.
acceptance_criteria: Given a user mapped to multiple companies, when I assign roles, then the roles assigned in one company are independent of those in another.; Given a user in company A with an admin role, when they access company B, then only company B's assigned roles apply.; Given a system user, when they belong to multiple companies, then their effective permissions are always evaluated within the active company context.

title: Enforce workforce-type role boundaries
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want employees and contractors to have distinct role catalogs, so that workforce-type boundaries are enforced when assigning roles.
acceptance_criteria: Given an employee, when I assign a role, then only employee-catalog roles are available.; Given a contractor, when I assign a role, then only contractor-catalog roles are available.; Given a role catalog, when I attempt to assign a role from the wrong workforce type, then the assignment is prevented.

title: Platform operations and support roles
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want to support tenant onboarding, troubleshoot configuration, and assist with approved data imports, so that tenants receive operational support without needing full super-admin rights.
acceptance_criteria: Given operations-admin access, when I perform onboarding support and configuration troubleshooting, then I can complete these tasks without super-admin privileges.; Given a data import request, when it is approved, then I can assist with the import under approval.; Given operations access, when I view logs and audit trails, then I can inspect them without modifying transactional data.

title: Platform security and compliance oversight
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want to view metadata-level audit logs across tenants and manage security policies and password rules, so that I can govern security and compliance without modifying transactional data.
acceptance_criteria: Given cross-tenant audit access, when I view audit logs, then I see metadata-level information only.; Given security policies and password rules, when I manage them, then the updated rules apply platform-wide.; Given a compliance inquiry, when it is raised, then I can support it without accessing transactional records.; Given my role, when I attempt a transactional data modification, then the action is not permitted.

title: Portfolio operational execution and read-only oversight
user_story: As a Portfolio Admin, I want to execute employee lifecycle, leave, onboarding, and exit tasks across assigned companies, as well as read-only reporting and compliance visibility, so that centralized HR execution and oversight are supported with the correct privilege boundaries.
acceptance_criteria: Given a Portfolio HR Operations User, when I perform lifecycle, leave, onboarding, and exit tasks, then they apply to assigned companies, without structural master data ownership unless granted.; Given a Portfolio HR Operations User, when I run operational reporting, then it spans only my assigned companies.; Given a Portfolio Read-Only / Auditor, when I access portfolio companies, then I can view reports, policy alignment, and compliance status but cannot perform transactional actions.
source: FR 5.3 (Portfolio HR Operations User; Portfolio Read-Only / Auditor)

title: Group reporting and consolidated visibility
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want to view consolidated reports across group companies and search a cross-company employee directory, so that group leadership has consolidated visibility without transactional or configuration rights.
acceptance_criteria: Given group-company access, when I open consolidated reports, then I see data aggregated across the group companies.; Given the cross-company directory, when I search, then I can find employees across authorized group companies.; Given a Group Reporting Viewer, when I use the system, then I have no transactional or configuration rights.; Given my role, when I attempt a transactional or configuration action, then it is denied.

title: Managerial team-scoped approvals and actions
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want managers to view their team members and approve leave, attendance, and onboarding steps within their scope, so that line and matrix managers can manage their people with scope-limited rights.
acceptance_criteria: Given a Department Head / People Manager, when they access the system, then they can view assigned team members, approve leave, attendance, and onboarding steps, participate in performance workflows, and initiate lifecycle actions.; Given a Functional / Project Manager, when they approve requests, then approval rights are limited to employees in their assigned functional groups.; Given a manager, when they attempt an action outside their team or functional scope, then it is denied.

title: Employee self-service and limited-access roles
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to view/update my profile, submit leave requests, view attendance, and access announcements and documents, so that I can manage my own HR interactions with policy-based limitations enforced where applicable.
acceptance_criteria: Given a Standard Employee, when I log in, then I can view and update my personal profile, submit leave, view attendance, access announcements and documents, and participate in feedback workflows.; Given a Limited-Access Employee, when I log in, then I have read-only access with limited transactions based on policy.; Given policy restrictions, when I attempt a disallowed transaction, then it is blocked.

title: Candidate and interview panel participation
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to participate in the talent acquisition process through panel membership, interview scheduling, and feedback submission, so that candidates can be evaluated prior to conversion to employees.
acceptance_criteria: Given a candidate managed in the talent acquisition module, when the candidate is processed, then they are handled as a prospective employee prior to conversion.; Given an Interview Panel Member, when assigned to a panel, then I can be scheduled for interviews and submit feedback.; Given a candidate is converted, when conversion completes, then they become an employee record in the target company.

title: System user identity model
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want a generic authenticated system user to belong to one or more companies and optionally be mapped to an employee, so that user, employee, and contractor remain distinct concepts across companies.
acceptance_criteria: Given a system user, when I configure their membership, then they may belong to one or more companies.; Given a user, when I map them, then they may be mapped to an employee, mapped across separate companies, or neither.; Given the model, when records are created, then User, Employee, and Contractor remain distinct concepts and an employee or contractor may exist without being a user.

title: Company-specific employee records and multi-company employment
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want employee records to always be company-specific with separate records per company for the same person, so that cross-company employment is modeled correctly.
acceptance_criteria: Given the same physical person employed in multiple companies, when records are created, then separate employee records exist in each company.; Given a person, when they are an employee in one company and a contractor in another, then both relationships are allowed simultaneously.; Given an employee, when created, then they belong to at least one department, hold one position, belong to one jurisdiction, and may operate across one or more locations.; Given an employee record, when accessed, then it is always company-specific and never shared across tenants.

title: Organizational structure and policy applicability rules
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want departments/groups to support n-level hierarchy, positions to belong to one department, locations to be company-specific (shareable in a group), and policies to apply across selected applicability dimensions, so that organizational and policy structures reflect real business rules.
acceptance_criteria: Given groups and departments, when I build the hierarchy, then n-level nesting is supported and each position belongs to exactly one department.; Given a company, when it operates across multiple jurisdictions, then this is allowed; locations are company-specific by default and shareable only among related companies via group-company relationships.; Given a policy, when I configure applicability, then it may apply to one, multiple, or all supported applicability dimensions.

title: Performance, scalability, and concurrency targets
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want the system to meet defined performance, scalability, and concurrency targets, so that tenants experience responsive service even at large scale and peak load.
acceptance_criteria: Given standard user interactions (page loads, forms, searches, navigation), when performed, then responses complete in under 2 seconds, including tenants up to 1000 employees.; Given reporting, when generated, then standard reports (up to 1000 records) complete within 30 seconds and large reports (1,000-10,000 records) within 2 minutes.; Given imports/exports, when run, then files up to 1000 records complete within 1 minute and up to 10,000 records within 5 minutes.; Given peak hours, when up to 20% of employees are concurrent with burst up to 2x normal load, then the system remains responsive; the system supports companies with thousands of users and there is no defined platform limit on users per company or companies per portfolio.

title: Availability, disaster recovery, and maintenance operations
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want defined availability, backup, disaster-recovery, and maintenance-window targets to be met, so that services remain reliable and recoverable with predictable downtime.
acceptance_criteria: Given service tiers, when measured, then uptime meets 99.99% (critical), 99.95% (standard), and 99.9% (background); active-passive multi-region with failover within 5 minutes and asynchronous cross-region replication.; Given a disaster, when recovery is invoked, then RTO is 4h (critical)/8h (standard) and RPO is 15 min (critical)/1h (standard).; Given the backup strategy, when executed, then continuous log streaming, hourly incrementals, daily fulls (30-day retention), weekly system backups (90-day), and monthly archival (7-year) are performed and DR is tested (quarterly tabletop, bi-annual failover, monthly restore tests).; Given maintenance, when scheduled, then it occurs at most once per month for up to 4 hours on Sunday 2:00-6:00 AM IST with 7 days advance notice.

title: Security, encryption, compliance, and data residency
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want encryption, secure development, compliance certifications, and data-residency requirements to be enforced, so that the platform protects data and meets regulatory obligations.
acceptance_criteria: Given sensitive data (PII, credentials, audit logs), when stored, then it is encrypted with AES-256; all transmission uses TLS 1.2+; all backups are encrypted.; Given the SDLC, when software is built, then secure coding, security-focused reviews, vulnerability management, penetration testing (application, infrastructure, API), and security monitoring with anomaly detection and incident response are applied.; Given compliance targets, when assessed, then the platform supports ISO 27001, SOC 2 (Type I Phase 1; Type II future), and GDPR/DPDP readiness.; Given Indian customers, when data is stored, then it resides in India unless otherwise contractually agreed.
source: FR 8.5 (Data Residency); FR 8.6 (Security, Compliance and Statutory Coverage)

title: Data governance, retention, and data subject rights
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want retention rules, deletion/anonymization, and data-subject-rights handling to be enforced, so that the platform complies with DPDP/GDPR obligations.
acceptance_criteria: Given each data type, when retained, then defined retention periods apply (active employee/contractor employment + 7 years; audit logs 1 year active + 6 years archived; completed workflows 7 years; notifications 1 year; rejected candidates 1 year; inactive candidates 6 months).; Given a terminated employee, when processed, then the user account is disabled immediately, the record stays active 30 days for exit processing, is anonymized after 30 days, and permanently deleted after 7 years, with automated monthly purge and legal-hold suspension.; Given anonymization, when applied, then direct identifiers are pseudonymized, indirect identifiers retained for reporting, and unique identifiers one-way hashed.; Given a data-subject request, when submitted, then Right to Access (machine-readable copy within 30 days), Rectification (self-service for non-sensitive fields, workflow-based for sensitive fields), and Erasure (with statutory exceptions) are supported via a self-service portal with identity verification, request tracking, SLA monitoring, and DPO support.

title: Global search, usability, and localization
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want tenant-aware global search, recently viewed records, and configurable locale formats, so that I can quickly find information in a usable, appropriately formatted interface.
acceptance_criteria: Given global search, when I search, then results span Employees, Candidates, and Document names while respecting tenant boundaries, including custom fields.; Given usability features, when I use the system, then recently viewed records are shown (default 10).; Given localization, when configured, then the UI is English-only for Phase 1 with configurable date, number, currency, and address formats at company or jurisdiction level.

title: API-first architecture, authentication integrations, and webhooks
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want an API-first platform with supported authentication integrations and configurable webhooks, so that the company can integrate SatelliteHR with external identity providers and downstream systems.
acceptance_criteria: Given Phase 1 integrations, when configured, then authentication providers SAML, AD, Office 365, and Google Apps are supported (authentication providers only for Phase 1).; Given the architecture, when UI functionality is used, then it is exposed via RESTful APIs (React frontend, .NET Core backend, PostgreSQL) with OAuth 2.0/JWT authentication and OpenAPI/Swagger documentation.; Given webhooks configured at company level, when events occur (employee lifecycle, leave requests, attendance exceptions, workflow changes, document uploads), then notifications are sent over HTTPS with signature verification.

title: Non-user employee and contractor records
user_story: As an Employee (Non-User), I want my employee or contractor record to be maintained by HR without requiring a system login, so that people who do not need platform access are still fully represented and administered.
acceptance_criteria: Given an employee or contractor who is not a system user, when HR creates the record, then it exists and is fully maintainable without any associated login.; Given a non-user employee, when HR later provisions access, then a system user can be mapped to the existing employee record without duplicating it.; Given a non-user record, when reports and lifecycle actions run, then the record is included the same as user-mapped employees; Given the identity model, when records are created, then an employee or contractor may exist without being a user and User/Employee/Contractor remain distinct concepts.

title: Company IT and security administration
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want company-level identity, access, and security administration covering user-role assignment, SSO/authentication configuration, and access audits, so that company access and security are governed without tenant-wide HR ownership.
acceptance_criteria: Given a Company IT / Security Admin, when I assign users to roles, then those assignments take effect within the company context.; Given company authentication, when I configure SSO and authentication settings, then the enabled methods apply to the company's users.; Given access governance, when I run access audits, then I can review who has which access within the company.; Given this role, when I attempt HR operations outside identity/access/security scope, then those actions are outside my remit.

title: Company finance and compliance view-only access
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want a company-level finance/compliance viewer with read-only access to employee, attendance, and leave data plus compliance and export reports, so that audit and statutory reporting are supported without HR operations access.
acceptance_criteria: Given a Company Finance / Compliance Viewer, when I access the system, then I can view employee, attendance, and leave data in read-only mode.; Given compliance needs, when I generate compliance and export reports, then I can produce them within the company tenant scope.; Given this role, when I attempt any HR operation or data modification, then the action is denied.

title: Immutable tenant-scoped audit trail persistence
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want an immutable, tenant-scoped, effective-dated audit trail persisted for security and data-change events, so that activity can be reconstructed and retained per policy across tenants.
acceptance_criteria: Given any security or data-change event, when it occurs, then an immutable audit record is persisted with tenant scope, actor, timestamp, and before/after context.; Given audit data at rest, when stored, then it is encrypted with AES-256 and logically isolated per tenant.; Given retention policy, when applied, then audit logs are kept 1 year active plus 6 years archived.; Given cross-tenant platform review, when audit logs are read, then only metadata-level information is exposed and records cannot be altered or deleted before retention expiry.
source: FR 8.6.2 (L1 data); FR 8.7.1 (audit log retention); FR 5.2 (Platform Security & Compliance Admin)

title: Per-tenant custom field (UDF) schema configuration
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to define per-tenant custom field (UDF) schemas as governed configuration, so that tenant-specific fields can be added without code and surfaced on forms and in search.
acceptance_criteria: Given custom field configuration, when I define a UDF schema (name, data type, applicability), then it is saved as per-tenant governed config without any code change.; Given a defined custom field, when forms render, then the field appears for data capture within the tenant scope.; Given global search, when I search, then custom fields are included in the searchable set while respecting tenant boundaries.; Given a schema change, when I update or version it, then existing records remain consistent and the new definition is effective-dated.

title: Notification and webhook delivery engine
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want the shared notification/template engine to evaluate configured webhook and notification subscriptions and dispatch them on subscribed events, so that downstream systems and recipients are reliably informed.
acceptance_criteria: Given configured webhook subscriptions, when a subscribed event occurs (employee lifecycle, leave requests, attendance exceptions, workflow changes, document uploads), then the engine dispatches a notification over HTTPS with signature verification.; Given a delivery attempt, when the endpoint is unavailable, then the engine handles delivery per its retry behavior.; Given notification records, when generated, then they are retained for 1 year per policy.; Given tenant scope, when the engine evaluates subscriptions, then only the owning company's events and endpoints are processed.

title: Versioned, effective-dated governed configuration
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want platform governed configuration (roles, permission sets, jurisdiction rule-packs, locale formats, and security/password policies) to be versioned and effective-dated, so that behavior can change without code deployment and history is preserved.
acceptance_criteria: Given governed configuration, when I change roles, permission sets, jurisdiction rule-packs, locale formats, or security/password rules, then the change takes effect without a code deployment.; Given a configuration change, when saved, then it is versioned and effective-dated so that prior versions remain reconstructable.; Given engines that read configuration, when they execute, then they interpret the currently effective configuration version.; Given tenant scope, when config is applied, then per-tenant overrides remain isolated to their company.
source: FR 5.1 (L2 config); FR 8.9 (locale formats); FR 5.2 (security policies)

title: Workflow and approval routing engine
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want a shared workflow/approval engine that routes lifecycle actions, sensitive-field changes, and cross-company sharing requests to the correct approvers, so that governed changes follow a consistent, auditable approval process.
acceptance_criteria: Given a governed change (employee lifecycle action, sensitive-field rectification, or cross-company sharing request), when it is initiated, then the engine routes it to the configured approver(s) and records its state.; Given an approval decision, when an approver acts, then the workflow advances (approved, rejected, or returned) and the change is applied only on final approval.; Given a completed workflow, when it finishes, then it is retained for 7 years per policy and a workflow-change event is emitted to subscribed webhooks.; Given tenant scope, when the engine routes and evaluates workflows, then it operates only within the owning company's context and respects authorized cross-company approval rules.
source: FR 4.4 (apply policies and workflows); FR 8.7.1 (completed workflows retention); FR 8.10.3 (workflow changes event) (L3 Workflow/Approval engine)

title: Employee self-service data subject rights
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to exercise my data subject rights (access, rectification, erasure) through a self-service portal, so that I can obtain, correct, or request deletion of my personal data in line with DPDP/GDPR.
acceptance_criteria: Given the self-service portal, when I request a copy of my data (Right to Access), then I receive a complete machine-readable export within 30 days.; Given a rectification request, when I edit non-sensitive fields, then changes apply immediately via self-service, while sensitive-field changes are routed through an approval workflow.; Given a Right to Erasure request, when I submit it, then my data is deleted or anonymized subject to statutory exception handling.; Given any request, when submitted, then my identity is verified and the request is tracked with SLA monitoring and DPO support.
source: FR 8.7.3 (Data Subject Rights) (L4 self-service; L3 Workflow/Approval)

title: User Employee and Contractor as distinct concepts
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want User, Employee, and Contractor to be modeled as distinct concepts, so that identity, employment, and engagement are never conflated.
acceptance_criteria: Given the data model, when records are created, then User, Employee, and Contractor are stored as three separate entities and are never merged into a single record.; Given a single physical person who is both a system user and an employee, when the records are related, then they are joined by an explicit user-to-employee mapping rather than conflated.; Given §7 rule 7, when a person needs no login, then an Employee or Contractor may exist with no associated User, and a User may exist mapped to no employee.; Given any operation that references a person, when it resolves the identity, then it selects the correct concept (User vs Employee vs Contractor) and remains scoped to the active company context.

title: User membership across multiple companies
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want a single user to belong to multiple companies, so that a person working with several tenants uses one identity.
acceptance_criteria: Given a single User identity, when access is granted to more than one company, then the same User is associated with each company without creating duplicate identities.; Given a multi-company User, when they operate within a company, then only that company's data and that company's assigned roles/permissions are in effect (per §7 rule 15).; Given a User already authenticated, when they move between authorized companies, then no re-authentication is required (per §7 rule 3).; Given access to one company is revoked, when the change is saved, then access to the User's other companies is unaffected.

title: Single-login administration of multiple companies
user_story: As a Portfolio Admin, I want to administer multiple companies through a single login and switch company context without repeated authentication, so that shared-services administration is seamless.
acceptance_criteria: Given an authenticated portfolio manager, when they switch between authorized companies, then no re-authentication is required.; Given a context switch, when it completes, then subsequent actions apply to the newly selected company only.; Given an unauthorized company, when a switch is attempted, then it is denied.

title: Employee record is always company-specific
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want every employee record to be company-specific, so that employee data never leaks across tenants.
acceptance_criteria: Given an employee record, when it is created, then it is owned by exactly one company and stamped with that company/tenant identifier.; Given a user of a different company, when they query or search, then the employee record is not visible and the cross-tenant request is denied.; Given reporting or export, when run, then employee records are scoped to the owning company only and are never aggregated across tenants except via authorized cross-company access.; Given an attempt to reassign an employee record to another tenant, when submitted, then it is rejected.

title: Separate employee records per company for one person
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want separate employee records to exist in each company when the same person is employed by multiple companies, so that each employment is administered independently.
acceptance_criteria: Given the same physical person employed by two companies, when records are created, then a distinct employee record exists in each company, each company-specific (per §7 rule 4).; Given one such record is updated, when the change is saved, then the record in the other company is unaffected.; Given termination in one company, when processed, then employment in the other company continues independently.; Given no cross-tenant identity link is exposed, when either record is viewed, then it reveals only its own company's data.

title: Concurrent employee and contractor engagements across companies
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want the same person to be an employee in one company and a contractor in another at the same time, so that mixed engagement types are supported.
acceptance_criteria: Given a person who is an employee in company A, when they are also engaged as a contractor in company B, then both relationships are valid simultaneously.; Given each engagement, when a role is assigned, then only the matching workforce-type catalog applies (employee-catalog vs contractor-catalog, per §5.1.3 / SYS-12).; Given one engagement ends, when processed, then the other engagement is unaffected.; Given each engagement, when accessed, then it remains scoped to its own company's tenant.

title: Employee or contractor without a user account
user_story: As an Employee (Non-User), I want to exist as an employee or contractor record without a system user account, so that people who do not need login are still administered.
acceptance_criteria: Given an employee or contractor, when HR creates the record, then it exists and is fully maintainable with no associated User account and no login.; Given a non-user record, when access is later required, then a User can be mapped to the existing record without creating a duplicate employee/contractor record.; Given HR processes (reporting, lifecycle actions, payroll inputs), when they run, then non-user records are included identically to user-mapped records.; Given the identity model, when records are created, then User/Employee/Contractor remain distinct concepts (per §7 rule 1).

title: Employee department and position assignment
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want every employee to belong to at least one department and hold exactly one position, so that org placement is always defined.
acceptance_criteria: Given an employee, when created, then they are assigned to at least one department (one or more allowed).; Given an employee, when created, then they hold exactly one position.; Given an attempt to save an employee with no department or with zero or more than one position, when submitted, then the save is rejected with a validation error.; Given the assigned position, when resolved, then it belongs to exactly one department (per §7 rule 9).

title: Position belongs to one department
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want each position to belong to exactly one department, so that the position catalog maps cleanly to the org structure.
acceptance_criteria: Given a position, when created, then it is linked to exactly one department.; Given an attempt to associate a position with multiple departments, when submitted, then it is rejected with a validation error.; Given a department reorganization, when positions are moved, then each position still belongs to exactly one department afterward.; Given department hierarchy is n-level (per §7 rule 13), when a position is placed, then it attaches to a single node in that hierarchy.

title: Employee jurisdiction and multi-location operation
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want each employee to belong to one jurisdiction and be able to operate across one or more locations, so that statutory and location assignments are accurate.
acceptance_criteria: Given an employee, when created, then they belong to exactly one jurisdiction.; Given an employee, when assigned locations, then they may operate across one or more locations.; Given the assigned jurisdiction, when rules are applied, then the corresponding jurisdiction rule-pack governs that employee.; Given the employee's company operates across multiple jurisdictions (per §7 rule 11), when the jurisdiction is chosen, then it must be one of the company's supported jurisdictions.

title: Company operating across multiple jurisdictions
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want a company to operate across multiple jurisdictions, so that multi-jurisdiction operations are supported within one tenant.
acceptance_criteria: Given a company, when configured, then it may be associated with more than one jurisdiction.; Given multiple jurisdictions, when employees are assigned, then each employee maps to exactly one jurisdiction from the company's supported set (per §7 rule 10).; Given jurisdiction rules, when applied, then the correct rule-pack applies per each employee's jurisdiction.; Given supported jurisdictions are drawn from the platform jurisdictions catalog (§4.1), when a company adds one, then only catalog jurisdictions are selectable.

title: Company-specific and group-shared locations
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want locations to be company-specific by default and shareable only among related companies via group-company relationships, so that location data respects tenant boundaries.
acceptance_criteria: Given a location, when created, then it is company-specific by default and isolated to its owning tenant.; Given a group-company relationship with shared locations enabled, when configured, then related companies may share a location subject to cross-company sharing approval (per §4.3).; Given companies with no group-company relationship, when one references another's location, then access is denied.; Given a shared location, when used, then tenant-boundary rules (§7 rule 15) still govern the underlying business data.

title: N-level hierarchy for groups and departments
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want groups and departments to support n-level hierarchy, so that complex organizational structures can be modeled.
acceptance_criteria: Given departments, when I build the structure, then arbitrary depth (n-level) nesting is supported.; Given groups, when I build the structure, then arbitrary depth (n-level) nesting is supported.; Given a nested node, when it is moved, then its entire sub-tree and parent/child relationships are preserved.; Given hierarchy integrity, when a change would create a cycle, then it is rejected.

title: Multi-dimensional policy applicability
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want policies to apply to one, multiple, or all supported applicability dimensions, so that policy targeting matches business needs.
acceptance_criteria: Given a policy, when I configure applicability, then I may target one, several, or all supported applicability dimensions.; Given overlapping policies, when evaluated for an entity, then the applicable policy is resolved consistently per the configured dimensions.; Given an all-dimensions policy, when applied, then it affects every applicable entity within the company.; Given policy scope, when evaluated, then it remains within the owning company's tenant boundary (per §7 rule 15).

title: Tenant-boundary enforcement on all data access
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want all data access to always respect tenant boundaries and authorized cross-company access rules, so that isolation is never bypassed.
acceptance_criteria: Given any data request, when processed, then tenant boundaries are enforced before any data is returned.; Given authorized cross-company access (multi-company user, portfolio construct, or group-company relationship), when configured, then only the explicitly permitted shared data is accessible (per §4.5).; Given an unauthorized cross-tenant request, when made, then it is denied and an audit record is logged.; Given audit review of denials, when read at platform level, then only metadata-level information is exposed (per §5.2).

title: Sub-2-second response for standard interactions
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want standard user interactions to respond in under 2 seconds, so that the application feels responsive.
acceptance_criteria: Given standard interactions (page loads, forms, searches, navigation), when performed, then responses complete in under 2 seconds.; Given normal load, when interactions are measured, then the sub-2-second target is met consistently.; Given a regression above 2 seconds, when detected, then it is flagged for remediation.

title: Sub-2-second response at 1000-employee tenant scale
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want tenants with up to 1000 employees to still receive sub-2-second responses, so that large tenants are not degraded.
acceptance_criteria: Given a tenant with up to 1000 employees, when standard interactions are performed, then responses complete in under 2 seconds.; Given the large-tenant benchmark, when executed, then the sub-2-second target is validated.; Given scale beyond the benchmark, when observed, then performance is monitored against the target.

title: Report generation within defined time limits
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want standard and large reports generated within defined time limits, so that reporting is timely.
acceptance_criteria: Given a standard report of up to 1000 records, when generated, then it completes within 30 seconds.; Given a large report of 1,000 to 10,000 records, when generated, then it completes within 2 minutes.; Given a report exceeding limits, when detected, then it is flagged for optimization.

title: Import and export within defined time limits
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want imports and exports to complete within defined time limits, so that bulk data operations are efficient.
acceptance_criteria: Given a file of up to 1000 records, when imported or exported, then it completes within 1 minute.; Given a file of up to 10,000 records, when imported or exported, then it completes within 5 minutes.; Given a file exceeding limits, when processed, then progress is reported and the operation is handled gracefully.

title: Concurrency and burst load handling
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want the system to handle peak concurrency and burst traffic, so that service stays stable under load.
acceptance_criteria: Given peak hours, when up to 20% of total employees are concurrent, then the system remains responsive.; Given burst traffic, when load reaches up to 2x normal, then the system continues to serve requests within targets.; Given sustained overload, when detected, then the system degrades gracefully without data loss.

title: Scalability to thousands of users without fixed limits
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want the system to scale to thousands of users with no fixed platform limits, so that customer growth is unconstrained.
acceptance_criteria: Given a company, when it grows, then the system supports companies with thousands of users.; Given the platform, when configured, then there is no defined limit on users per company or on companies per portfolio.; Given increasing scale, when load rises, then capacity scales to meet demand while sub-2-second targets (§8.1) continue to hold up to the 1000-employee benchmark.; Given burst traffic up to 2x normal load at peak (§8.1 rule 5), when it occurs, then the system continues to serve requests within targets.

title: Tiered uptime availability targets
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want tiered uptime targets to be met, so that services meet their availability commitments.
acceptance_criteria: Given critical services, when measured over the period, then uptime is at least 99.99%.; Given standard services, when measured, then uptime is at least 99.95%.; Given background services, when measured, then uptime is at least 99.9%.

title: Multi-region active-passive failover
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want an active-passive multi-region architecture with rapid failover, so that a regional outage does not cause prolonged downtime.
acceptance_criteria: Given a regional failure, when failover is triggered, then the passive region takes over within 5 minutes.; Given cross-region data, when replicated, then asynchronous replication is used.; Given failover completion, when validated, then service resumes in the secondary region.

title: Controlled maintenance windows
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want maintenance windows to be controlled and pre-announced, so that planned downtime is predictable.
acceptance_criteria: Given maintenance, when scheduled, then it occurs at most once per month for up to 4 hours.; Given the window, when set, then it falls on Sunday 2:00-6:00 AM IST.; Given a planned window, when announced, then customers receive at least 7 days advance notice.

title: Recovery time and recovery point objectives
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want defined RTO and RPO to be met, so that recovery from disasters is bounded.
acceptance_criteria: Given a disaster affecting critical services, when recovery runs, then RTO is within 4 hours and RPO is within 15 minutes.; Given standard services, when recovery runs, then RTO is within 8 hours and RPO is within 1 hour.; Given a recovery event, when completed, then achieved RTO/RPO are measured against targets.

title: Tiered backup strategy with retention
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want a tiered backup strategy with defined retention, so that data can be restored to appropriate points in time.
acceptance_criteria: Given backups, when executed, then continuous transaction log streaming, hourly incrementals, and daily full backups (30-day retention) are performed.; Given longer-term backups, when executed, then weekly system backups (90-day retention) and monthly archival (7-year retention) are performed.; Given a restore request, when made, then data is restored from the appropriate backup tier.

title: Disaster-recovery testing cadence
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want a regular DR testing cadence, so that recovery procedures are proven.
acceptance_criteria: Given DR readiness, when validated, then quarterly tabletop exercises are conducted.; Given failover capability, when validated, then bi-annual failover tests are conducted.; Given backups, when validated, then monthly automated backup restoration tests are conducted.

title: India data residency for target customers
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want customer data for India-based customers to reside in India, so that residency expectations are met.
acceptance_criteria: Given a primary target customer in India, when data is stored, then it resides in India.; Given a contractual exception, when agreed, then residency may differ only as contractually specified.; Given data placement, when audited, then residency compliance can be demonstrated.

title: Compliance certification readiness
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want the platform to support key compliance certifications, so that regulatory and customer assurance needs are met.
acceptance_criteria: Given ISO 27001, when the platform is assessed, then it is designed to support compliance.; Given SOC 2, when scoped, then Type I applies for Phase 1 and Type II is planned for a future phase.; Given GDPR and DPDP, when assessed, then the platform is GDPR-ready and supports India DPDP obligations.

title: Encryption at rest, in transit, and for backups
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want data encrypted at rest, in transit, and in backups, so that sensitive data is protected everywhere.
acceptance_criteria: Given sensitive data (PII, credentials, audit logs) at rest, when stored, then it is encrypted using AES-256.; Given data in transit, when transmitted, then TLS 1.2 or higher is used.; Given backups, when created, then they are encrypted.

title: Secure development and vulnerability management
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want secure development practices and ongoing security testing, so that vulnerabilities are minimized and managed.
acceptance_criteria: Given the SDLC, when software is built, then security requirements, secure coding, and security-focused code reviews are applied.; Given vulnerabilities, when found, then regular assessments, timely patching, and disclosure/remediation processes apply, including application, infrastructure, and API penetration testing.; Given operations, when running, then security event monitoring, anomaly detection, and incident response procedures are in place.

title: Data retention periods by data type
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want retention periods enforced per data type, so that data is kept only as long as required.
acceptance_criteria: Given active employee/contractor data, when retained, then it is kept for duration of employment plus 7 years.; Given audit logs, when retained, then 1 year active plus 6 years archived, completed workflows 7 years, and notifications 1 year.; Given candidate data, when retained, then rejected candidates are kept 1 year and inactive candidates 6 months.

title: Deletion, anonymization, and data purge
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want terminated-employee deletion, anonymization, and automated purge to be enforced, so that data lifecycle obligations are met.
acceptance_criteria: Given a terminated employee, when processed, then the user account is disabled immediately, the record stays active 30 days for exit processing, is anonymized after 30 days, and permanently deleted after 7 years.; Given anonymization, when applied, then direct identifiers are pseudonymized, indirect identifiers retained for reporting, and unique identifiers one-way hashed.; Given purge, when scheduled, then an automated monthly purge runs with legal-hold suspension capability.

title: Data subject rights fulfillment
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to exercise access, rectification, and erasure rights, so that my data protection rights are honored.
acceptance_criteria: Given a Right to Access request, when submitted, then a complete machine-readable data copy is provided within 30 days.; Given a Right to Rectification, when requested, then non-sensitive fields are self-service editable and sensitive fields go through a workflow.; Given a Right to Erasure, when requested, then data is deleted or anonymized with statutory exception handling, supported by a self-service portal with identity verification, request tracking, SLA monitoring, and DPO support.

title: Tenant-aware global search
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want global search across employees, candidates, and document names, so that I can quickly find records within my tenant.
acceptance_criteria: Given global search, when I search, then results span Employees, Candidates, and Document names.; Given tenant boundaries, when results are returned, then only records within my tenant are shown.; Given a cross-tenant match, when it exists, then it is excluded from my results.

title: Recently viewed records and custom-field search
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want recently viewed records and custom fields included in search, so that navigation is faster and complete.
acceptance_criteria: Given recently viewed records, when I return, then the default 10 most recent records are shown.; Given custom fields, when I search, then custom fields are included in the search scope.; Given tenant boundaries, when recent items and custom-field results are shown, then they respect tenant isolation.

title: Language and configurable locale formats
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want English-only Phase 1 with configurable locale formats, so that data is displayed appropriately per company or jurisdiction.
acceptance_criteria: Given Phase 1, when the UI renders, then English is the only supported language.; Given locale settings, when configured, then date, number, currency, and address formats are configurable at company or jurisdiction level.; Given a configured locale, when data is displayed, then it follows the configured formats.

title: Phase 1 authentication provider integrations
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want supported authentication provider integrations, so that users can sign in via existing identity systems.
acceptance_criteria: Given Phase 1 integration scope, when configured, then SAML, AD, Office 365, and Google Apps authentication providers are supported.; Given Phase 1, when other external integrations are requested, then only authentication providers are in scope.; Given a configured provider, when a user signs in, then authentication is delegated to that provider.

title: API-first architecture with documented REST APIs
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want an API-first architecture exposing all UI functionality via secured REST APIs, so that the platform is integrable and consistent.
acceptance_criteria: Given the architecture, when built, then it uses a React frontend, .NET Core backend, and PostgreSQL database.; Given UI functionality, when delivered, then it is exposed via RESTful APIs secured with OAuth 2.0/JWT authentication.; Given the APIs, when published, then OpenAPI/Swagger documentation is available.

title: Company-configurable secure webhooks
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want configurable webhooks for key events with secure delivery, so that downstream systems receive reliable event notifications.
acceptance_criteria: Given webhooks, when configured at company level, then they can subscribe to employee lifecycle, leave requests, attendance exceptions, workflow changes, and document upload events.; Given an event, when it fires, then the webhook is delivered over HTTPS with signature verification.; Given tenant scope, when events are dispatched, then only the owning company's events and endpoints are used.
